topic.name: Chemical Elements and Periodic Trends
topic.description: This topic encompasses the classification, properties, and trends of chemical elements within the periodic table, focusing on specific groups and periods.

Document 1: The actinide series encompasses the 15 metallic chemical elements with atomic numbers from 89 to 103
All actinides are radioactive and release energy upon radioactive decay.
Document 2: A period 1 element is one of the chemical elements in the first row of the periodic table
The first period contains fewer elements than any other row in the table, with only two: hydrogen and helium.
Document 3: The alkali metals are a group in the periodic table consisting of the chemical elements lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, caesium, and francium
The alkali metals provide the best example of group trends in properties in the periodic table.

topic.name: Singapore Administrative Law
topic.description: This topic encompasses the principles and doctrines governing judicial review and administrative decision-making in Singapore, including standing, legitimate expectations, and the exclusion of judicial review.

Document 1: Threshold issues are legal requirements in Singapore administrative law that must be satisfied by applicants before their claims for judicial review
Applicants must establish they have private standing if they seek to represent personal interests.
Document 2: The doctrine of legitimate expectation in Singapore protects both procedural and substantive rights

The recognition of substantive legitimate expectations is somewhat controversial as it requires a balancing of the requirements of fairness against the reasons for any change in the authority's policy.
Document 3: Exclusion of judicial review has been attempted by the Parliament of Singapore to protect the exercise of executive power
The courts there have taken the view that such clauses are, subject to some exceptions, not effective in denying or restricting the extent to which the courts are able to exercise judicial review.

topic.name: Pennsylvania Tributaries
topic.description: The documents discuss various tributaries in Pennsylvania, focusing on their characteristics, courses, and ecological significance.

Document 1: Harveys Creek is a tributary of the Susquehanna River in Luzerne County, Pennsylvania
The watershed is designated as a High-Quality Coldwater Fishery
Document 2: Messers Run is a tributary of Catawissa Creek in Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
The creek is a High-Quality Coldwater Fishery and Class A Wild Trout Waters in some places
Document 3: Little Catawissa Creek is a tributary of Catawissa Creek in Columbia County and Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
The creek is a High-Quality Coldwater Fishery and Class A Wild Trout Waters in some places

topic.name: Horse Breeds and Characteristics
topic.description: This topic encompasses various horse breeds, their origins, characteristics, and uses.

Document 1: The Breton is a breed of draft horse.
There are three distinct subtypes of the Breton.
Document 2: The Kentucky Mountain Saddle Horse is a horse breed from the US state of Kentucky.
Developed as an all-around farm and riding horse in eastern Kentucky.
Document 3: The Welara is a part-Arabian pony breed developed from the Arabian horse and the Welsh pony.
They are used for many disciplines of English riding.

topic.name: WWE Pay-Per-View Events
topic.description: The documents discuss various professional wrestling pay-per-view events produced by WWE, highlighting matches, outcomes, and notable occurrences.

Document 1: Cyber Sunday was the fourth annual Cyber Sunday professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
The main match on the SmackDown! brand was Batista versus The Undertaker for the World Heavyweight Championship.
Document 2: Vengeance: Night of Champions was the seventh annual professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).
Every match on the card was contested for a championship; two were won and seven were retained.
Document 3: Judgment Day was the ninth annual Judgment Day professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).

topic.name: Hampshire County Politics and Media
topic.description: This topic encompasses the political history and media representation of Hampshire County, Virginia (now West Virginia), focusing on prominent figures and publications from the 19th century.

1: Alexander W. Monroe was a prominent American lawyer, politician, and military officer in the U.S. states of Virginia and West Virginia.
Monroe served as a member of the Virginia House of Delegates (1850 – 1851 and 1862 – 1865) and West Virginia House of Delegates (1875 – 1877 and 1879 – 1883) representing Hampshire County.
2: The Virginia Argus and Hampshire Advertiser, often referred to simply as the Virginia Argus, was a weekly newspaper published between July 1850 and August 1861 in Romney, Virginia (now West Virginia).
Among the newspaper's proprietors was Alexander W. Monroe, a prominent Romney lawyer who had previously served as a member of the Virginia House of Delegates (1850 – 1851).
3: Isaac Parsons served as a member of the Virginia House of Delegates representing Hampshire County from 1789 until his death in 1796.
Parsons was the grandfather of Isaac Parsons (1814 – 1862), who also represented Hampshire County in the Virginia House of Delegates.

topic.name: Russian Naval Monitors
topic.description: The documents discuss the design, specifications, and service history of Uragan-class monitors built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the mid-1860s.

Document 1: Russian monitor Latnik was an Uragan-class monitor built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the mid-1860s.
She was one of two ships of the class to be built in Belgium and assembled in Russia.
Document 2: Russian monitor Edinorog was an Uragan-class monitor built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the mid-1860s.
The design was based on the American Passaic-class monitor, but was modified to suit Russian engines, guns and construction techniques.

topic.name: Educational Systems and History
topic.description: The documents explore the historical development and structure of educational systems in various regions, including Harvard Extension School, Iceland, and the Faroe Islands.

Document 1: The history of the Harvard Extension School dates back to its founding in 1910 by Abbott Lawrence Lowell.
The Harvard Extension School has been a leader in distance education, offering courses on the radio and television.
Document 2: Education in Iceland is divided in four levels: playschool, compulsory, upper secondary and higher.
Education is mandatory for children aged 6 – 16.
Document 3: The levels of education in the Faroe Islands are primary, secondary and higher education.
Education is compulsory for 9 years between the ages of 7 and 16.

topic.description: This topic encompasses the notable rivalries and competitive dynamics among top professional tennis players, particularly focusing on the achievements and interactions of the 'Big Four'.

topic.name: Mechanical-Electrical Analogies
topic.description: The documents discuss the principles and applications of mechanical-electrical analogies, particularly focusing on impedance and antimetric networks in the context of system analysis.

The impedance analogy is one of the two main mechanical-electrical analogies used for representing mechanical systems in the electrical domain.
Document 3: An antimetric electrical network is an electrical network that exhibits anti-symmetrical electrical properties.
References to symmetry and antimetry of a network usually refer to the input impedances of a two-port network when correctly terminated.

topic.name: South-West Indian Ocean Cyclone Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various seasons of tropical cyclones in the South-West Indian Ocean, detailing their formation, intensity, and impacts.

Document 1: The 1991 – 92 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was an average cyclone season in which most storms remained over open waters.
The basin was most active in February, when five named storms developed.
Document 2: The 1994 – 95 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was fairly active, with storms forming regularly from October through April.
Most of the storms originated from the intertropical convergence zone.
Document 3: The 1992 – 93 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season lasted longer than normal, with the first storm Aviona forming on September 27.
Tropical cyclones in this basin are monitored by the Regional Specialised Meteorological Centre in Réunion.

topic.name: Euro Banknotes Overview
topic.description: This topic covers the characteristics, history, and security features of various euro banknotes introduced in 2002.

Document 1: The ten euro note ( € 10 ) is the second-lowest value euro banknote and has been used since the introduction of the euro ( in its cash form ) in 2002.
The ten euro banknotes depict bridges and arches / doorways in Romanesque architecture.
Document 2: The one hundred euro note ( € 100 ) is one of the higher value euro banknotes and has been used since the introduction of the euro ( in its cash form ) in 2002.
The hundred euro notes depict bridges and arches / doorways in the Baroque and Rococo style.
Document 3: The two hundred euro note ( € 200 ) is the second-highest value euro banknote and has been used since the introduction of the euro ( in its cash form ) in 2002.
The € 200 note contains several complex security features such as watermarks, invisible ink, holograms and microprinting that document its authenticity.

topic.name: Pulp Science Fiction Magazines

Document 3: Wonder Story Annual was a science fiction pulp magazine which was launched in 1950.

topic.name: Low-Pressure Systems in Meteorology
topic.description: The topic covers the formation, characteristics, and effects of low-pressure areas in the atmosphere, particularly in relation to weather patterns and cyclogenesis.

Document 1: A low-pressure area, low or depression, is a region where the atmospheric pressure is lower than that of surrounding locations.
The formation process of a low-pressure area is known as cyclogenesis.
Document 2: Thermal lows, or heat lows, are non-frontal low-pressure areas that occur over the continents in the subtropics during the warm season.
The resulting hotter air is less dense than surrounding cooler air.

topic.name: Ancient Greek Wars
topic.description: The documents discuss significant battles and sieges involving Greek city-states and their conflicts with foreign powers, particularly Persia and Rome.

Document 1: Battle of Gythium was fought in 195 BC between Sparta and the coalition of Rome, Rhodes, the Achaean League and Pergamum.
The result of this battle forced Nabis, the tyrant of Sparta, to abandon the surrounding land.
Document 2: The Siege of Eretria took place in 490 BC, during the first Persian invasion of Greece.

An arranged album entitled Final Fantasy V Dear Friends... was released by Square / NTT Publishing.
Document 3: The music of the video games Final Fantasy and Final Fantasy II was composed by regular series composer Nobuo Uematsu.
All Sounds of Final Fantasy I • II, a compilation of almost all of the music in the games, was released by DataM / Polystar in 1989.

topic.name: Fictional Television Characters
topic.description: The documents discuss various fictional characters from popular television series and their development within the narratives.

Document 1: Ana Lucia Cortez is a fictional character on the ABC television series Lost
Ana Lucia becomes the leader of the tail section.
Document 2: Castiel is a fictional character portrayed by Misha Collins on the CW Television Network's American television series Supernatural
Castiel develops friendships with both men.
Document 3: Benjamin 'Ben' Linus is a fictional character portrayed by Michael Emerson on the ABC television series Lost
Ben was the leader of a group of island natives called the Others.

topic.name: Pop Concert Tours
topic.description: The documents discuss notable concert tours by prominent pop artists, highlighting their themes, setlists, and public reception.

Document 1: Re-Invention World Tour was the sixth concert tour by American singer-songwriter Madonna.
The tour garnered positive reception from contemporary critics.
Document 2: Baby One More Time Tour was the debut concert tour by American recording artist Britney Spears.
The tour received positive feedback from critics; many highlighted Spears' persona and edgy look.
Document 3: The You Drive Me Crazy Tour was the second concert tour by American recording artist Britney Spears.
During the tour, Spears was accused of lip synching, although she denied these claims.

topic.name: Major League Baseball Players
topic.description: The documents discuss the careers and biographies of various former Major League Baseball players.

Document 1: Dave Martinez is an American professional baseball coach and former outfielder.
He played in MLB for the Cubs, Montreal Expos, Cincinnati Reds, and several other teams.
Document 2: Bill Stein is a retired professional baseball player and manager.
His playing career spanned 17 seasons, 14 of which were spent in Major League Baseball.
Document 3: Daniel Thomas Meyer is a retired professional baseball player whose career spanned 15 seasons.
He primarily played first base, but also played left field, third base, and right field.

topic.name: Bicycle and Motorcycle Mechanics
topic.description: The dynamics and mechanics involved in the operation and performance of bicycles and motorcycles.

Document 1: Bicycle kick is a physical move in association football.
Its successful performance has largely been limited to the most experienced and athletic players.
Document 2: Bicycle and motorcycle dynamics is the science of the motion of bicycles and motorcycles and their components.
The study of these motions began in the late 19th century and continues today.
